qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PDF merge and split routes accept attacker-chosen local watermark and stamp paths
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is in the PDF engines merge and split watermark or stamp path handling (`pkg/modules/pdfengines`, `pkg/modules/pdfcpu`, and `pkg/modules/pdftk`), while the advisory is a Chromium `extraHttpHeaders` scope regex ReDoS in `pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go`; the entrypoints, vulnerable code, sink, and impact do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: compiling user-supplied `extraHttpHeaders` scope regexes in Chromium without a `MatchTimeout`, which enables catastrophic backtracking and worker hangs on `/forms/chromium/screenshot/url`.
evidence: The advisory details and fix commit `cfb48d9af48cb236244eabe5c67fe1d30fb3fe25` both point to `pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go`, where `regexp2.Compile(scope, regexp2.None)` gained `p.MatchTimeout = 5 * time.Second` for `extraHttpHeaders` scope handling.

The only final qualifying finding targets `POST /forms/pdfengines/merge` and `POST /forms/pdfengines/split`, with code locations in `pkg/modules/pdfengines/routes.go`, `pkg/modules/pdfcpu/pdfcpu.go`, and `pkg/modules/pdftk/pdftk.go`.
Its reproduced behavior is local file inclusion into a returned PDF via attacker-chosen watermark or stamp paths, not regex backtracking, Chromium header scoping, or a denial-of-service worker hang.
